page: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache resources include 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64s_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities, highlighted by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 21795.0 and a strong Passmark CPU score of 73628.05. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8), which contribute to strong performance in LLM inference tasks, such as processing 3294.26 tokens per second on a small 135M model. Memory performance is robust, with cached read bandwidth reaching 4665.0 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth at 110.72 GB/sec.

The D64s_v6 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for enterprise-scale deployments. Its qualitative cost efficiency is driven by high resource density and dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for workloads requiring consistent compute performance without the overhead of dedicated hardware. Given its top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and image processing, this server is well-suited for relational databases, high-throughput web servers, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, a 260 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities. The instance delivers top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and image processing, alongside strong multi-core capabilities in software compilation and LLM inference. Its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation provide a cost-effective, high-density resource profile for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for relational databases, web serving, and multi-threaded application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v6 is a 64-vCPU general-purpose VM with 256 GB RAM and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and balanced memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
Advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX) for accelerated workloads
Dedicated CPU allocation with 260 MB L3 cache and Hyper-V virtualization
Premium Storage capable configuration without local storage or onboard GPUs
